subject: Implement Authentication Context and Hooks
description: Create src/contexts/AuthContext.tsx with AuthProvider wrapping app, providing: user (User | null), session (Session | null), loading (boolean), signInWithGoogle(), signOut(), isAuthenticated. Use useEffect to listen to auth state changes via supabase.auth.onAuthStateChange(). Create src/hooks/useAuth.ts returning context value. Create src/hooks/useSession.ts for session-specific operations (refresh, check expiry). Handle loading states during initial auth check. Implement proper cleanup of auth listeners on unmount. Store user profile in context after fetching from public.users table.
result_summary:
Implemented the shared auth state layer and committed it as `4fdf2d3` (`[task-013] Implement auth context and hooks`). Added `src/contexts/AuthContext.tsx` with `AuthProvider`, session restoration via `supabase.auth.getSession()`, live updates via `supabase.auth.onAuthStateChange()`, Google OAuth sign-in using PKCE redirect targets, sign-out, and profile fetching from `public.users` with `UserSchema` validation before storing it in context. Added `src/hooks/useAuth.ts` and `src/hooks/useSession.ts`, exported them through the existing barrels, and updated `App.tsx` to wrap the app with `ThemeProvider` and `AuthProvider` so the new context is active in the running app.

Verification: `npm run typecheck` passed; `npm run lint -- App.tsx src/contexts/AuthContext.tsx src/contexts/index.ts src/hooks/useAuth.ts src/hooks/useSession.ts src/hooks/index.ts` passed (aside from ESLint’s expected eslintrc deprecation warning from the repo tooling bridge); `SUPABASE_URL=https://example.supabase.co SUPABASE_ANON_KEY=public-anon-key npm run build:web` passed. `git pull --rebase` was attempted first and failed because the shared worktree contained unrelated unstaged changes; the auth files were then committed directly without touching those unrelated changes.

Definition of Done notes: external data loaded from `public.users` is validated with `UserSchema`; authentication is handled by restoring and tracking the Supabase session; authorization for profile reads continues to rely on the existing Supabase session plus RLS policies. This task does not add a server endpoint, so request-param validation and server-side error status handling were not applicable. No dedicated automated happy-path/error-path auth test harness exists in the repo yet, so validation was limited to lint, typecheck, web export, manual inspection, and the commit.
